mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows. It is ideal for teams looking to build and deploy AI applications with minimal overhead. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dify Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.dify.ai

```

> This is the official repository for Dify Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Dify to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> Ensure CRDs are installed before deploying Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Dify in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace 0.12.0 with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource to configure Dify.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

```

> Replace 'example-workflow' with your actual workflow configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Dify Custom Resource to ensure it is ready.

```bash

kubectl get dify -n dify

```

> Look for the status to be 'Ready'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes Dify and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This will remove the Dify release.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations. This step will upgrade Dify to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get dify -n dify -o yaml > dify-backup.yaml

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.1

kubectl get dify -n dify

```

> Backup the current Dify configuration.
> Replace 0.12.1 with the desired version.
> Verify the Dify Custom Resource is in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get dify -n dify -o yaml > dify-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dify not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Dify Custom Resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Dify pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dify'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs may not have been applied correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Workflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified workflow in the Custom Resource does not exist.

**Fix:**
Ensure the workflow name in the Custom Resource matches an existing workflow.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dify pods are crashing'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be insufficient resources allocated to the Dify pods.

**Fix:**
Check resource limits and requests in the Dify deployment and adjust accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new workflow types and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old workflow configuration format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your existing workflows to the new format as per the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify Custom Resource in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get dify -n dify'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.ai/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
